title: Let’s do a peek inside the admin dashboard - Abuse API endpoint
snippet: Application is vulnerable to broken access control allows employee to become the admin of a workspace.Let’s do peek inside admin dashboard: Abuse API endpoint HeaderApplication introductionIt is a ...
content: Application is vulnerable to broken access control allows employee to become the admin of a workspace.Let’s do peek inside admin dashboard: Abuse API endpoint HeaderApplication introductionIt is a productivity app used by companies to track employee time and expenses.Key FeaturesThere are many features, but these are some of the important ones:  User can join many workspaces  User can create their own workspace  The admin of a workspace can invite other user by emailHow does application works?A user can join many workspaces from one account, but the user ID will be the same across all the workspaces. So, the user can create personal workspace along with the company’s workspace.This is what the request look a like:Request StructureThis is how Workspace ID look a like:Workspace ID table  While the test for role-based broken access control, Use PwnFox to colorize the tab and burpsuite proxy history.Limited funtions are visible to the user. Admin has access to all functionalities. I tried to change the workspace ID but it redirected me to homepage. All I can do is force browsing to access all the different functionalities.As I went through the various features as administrator, I tried to match and replace the Personal Workspace ID with the Enterprise Workspace ID.This is the final result when the user match and replace the personal workspace ID with the Company’s ID.Path differnceStep to ReproduceStep to reproduceEnd NoteIf you have enjoyed this article, Share this with your friends. Thank you!https://linktr.ee/bhavikkanejiyaReferences  Automating IDORs with Autorize - Common API Bugs Pt. 2 - IDORs & Access Control Issues by @Asseem_shrey  PimpMyBurp #1 – PwnFox + Autorize  Burp Suite tutorial: IDOR vulnerability automation using Autorize and AutoRepeater  Two account takeover bugs worth $$$$
